source.title: incident-bot: lightweight diagnostic toolbelt + ephemeral debug sessions for responders
source.author: Aisha Kaur
source.description: Motivation: on-call responders repeatedly run the same set of manual diagnostics when an alert fires (span tailing, recent-error log windows, lightweight heap/alloc snapshots, and quick-curl checks). This PR introduces a compact 'diagnostic toolbelt' exposed by incident-bot and an ephemeral debug session mode that lets responders run short-lived, auditable diagnostics without escalation to infra. Goals: reduce MTTD by providing one-click diagnostics, keep operations safe via TTLs and RBAC checks, and ensure cost caps through soft quotas and telemetry. What this change does: 1) Adds a new /v1/incidents/{id}/toolbelt API surface that aggregates a configurable set of diagnostics (logs tail, span tail, recent-errors, quick-rerun of failing healthcheck endpoints, and a heap-lite snapshot request). 2) Implements ephemeral debug sessions: a generated session token with a 15-minute TTL that grants scoped read-only access to targeted logs/traces and a sandboxed runner to perform pre-approved diagnostic commands. Sessions are recorded to the audit log and emit a session lifecycle event for billing/telemetry. 3) New UI affordance: a Toolbelt button in the incident timeline that opens the toolbelt panel (web/src/components/toolbelt/*) and a compact modal for ephemeral session creation with an estimated cost meter. 4) CLI helpers: incident-botctl toolbelt start --incident <id> --profile quick-check that opens a short-lived session and prints the session token and telemetry link. 5) Safety and Governance: RBAC checks against SSO groups, KMS-wrapped session secrets, per-tenant soft quotas, request rate-limiting, and a conservative whitelist of allowed runner commands. 6) Observability: new metrics (toolbelt.sessions.created, toolbelt.diagnostics.count, toolbelt.session.duration) and tracing spans to measure tool impact on latency/free resources. Implementation notes: backend changes in cmd/api/handler_toolbelt.go and internal/toolbelt/* which provide the orchestration, session manager, and audit plumbing; UI changes in web/src/components/toolbelt and web/src/styles; lightweight runner implemented as a new containerless job executor (internal/runner/lightweight) that runs commands in a hardened sandbox (no outbound network by default except to whitelisted endpoints). Diffs intentionally keep payloads small; large log downloads are paginated and pre-signed links have a short expiry. Testing: unit tests added for session lifecycle and RBAC checks (internal/toolbelt/session_test.go), e2e harness extends incident-sim to validate TTL expiry and audit log entries (test/e2e/toolbelt_flow.yaml). Migration and rollout: feature gated behind a runtime flag INCIDENT_TOOLBELT_ENABLED false by default; small canary rollout to 10% of orgs for first week with debug logging; telemetry hooks added to detect anomalous usage patterns and auto-disable soft-quotas if hit. Rollback plan: flip INCIDENT_TOOLBELT_ENABLED off, invalidate active sessions via session manager kill-all, and remove UI button via a targeted client config flag. References: ENG-4127 (design doc), ENG-4198 (safety review), and an internal runbook template updated to include toolbelt guidance.
commits: a1b3f9d: add toolbelt API and session manager
b2c5e41: implement lightweight runner and sandbox policies
c3d7f05: UI components for toolbelt modal and panel
d4e8f21: incident-botctl CLI helpers and docs
e5f901a: add unit and e2e tests, metrics and tracing
review_comments: Jordan Blake: Looks good overall but we need a stronger guarantee that the runner cannot make data exfiltration requests. Please add egress deny-by-default and an allowlist at the tenant-level.
Aisha Kaur: Added deny-by-default egress rule and tenant-level allowlist; updated tests to assert egress blocked for disallowed domains.
Lina Perez: Suggest adding a UI indicator for session scope (what resources are accessible) before creating a session. Also request a telemetry event for cancelled sessions.
Aisha Kaur: Added scope summary to the modal and emit toolbelt.session.cancel events; updated docs with the scope table.
Marcus Chen: e2e flake observed on session expiry; looks like clock skew causes premature TTL failures in test infra. Please add clock-sync tolerance in the test harness.
Aisha Kaur: Increased tolerance to 5s in e2e harness and retried; CI green.
ci_checks: ci/e2e: pass (flaky run fixed on rerun)
source.merge_summary: Squash-merged after approvals from Jordan and Lina. Small follow-ups tracked in ENG-4251 (UI polish) and ENG-4260 (runner instrumentation). Release note: Added an on-call diagnostic toolbelt and ephemeral debug sessions for incident responders, gated by feature flag and tenant quotas.
release_notes: On-call diagnostic toolbelt (incidents UI & API): one-click diagnostics including log tail, span tail, quick health checks, and lightweight heap snapshots.
Ephemeral debug sessions: short-lived, auditable sessions for responders with TTL, RBAC, and quotas.
Feature is gated behind INCIDENT_TOOLBELT_ENABLED and rolled out to 10% of orgs initially.

source.title: adaptive-signal-attenuator-and-stepwise-playbook-generator
source.author: Samira Alvarez
source.description: Motivation: On-call noise and low-signal alerts keep interrupting responders and dilute attention away from high-impact incidents. This PR introduces two complementary features: (1) an adaptive signal attenuator that temporarily mutes or down-prioritizes low-signal alert bursts based on multivariate heuristics (entity cardinality, recent noise history, error-rate delta), and (2) a stepwise playbook generator that converts canonical runbook templates into prioritized, step-by-step remediation checklists with embedded conditional telemetry checks. Both are exposed via the incident-bot core and a new CLI surface (oncallctl) and are designed for safe gradual rollout with canary attenuation and an explicit override flow.
source.summary: Adds adaptive signal-attenuator (rate/entropy-based suppression) + stepwise-playbook-generator (templated, telemetry-aware playbooks). CLI commands: oncallctl attenuate, oncallctl compose-playbook. Integrates with snapshot composer to attach a minimal telemetry bundle when playbooks are created.
commits: f3a1c02: feat(attenuator): initial adaptive attenuator implementation and API
b9d4e77: feat(playbook): add stepwise playbook renderer and telemetry checks
8c7b412: feat(cli): oncallctl commands for attenuate / compose-playbook
54a3d21: docs: add operational guide and rollout playbook (docs/oncall/attenuator.md)
1e2d5ff: test: unit + integration tests for attenuator heuristics and CLI
diff_snippets: internal/attenuator/attenuator.go: added AdaptiveWindow struct and Evaluate(signal) -> Action
review_comments: Ethan Li: Nice approach. Two requests: (1) add a CLI --dry-run that prints predicted attenuation decisions, (2) make sure the attenuator exposes a reason code for UI display so responders understand suppression rationale.
Samira Alvarez: Added --dry-run and reason_code on the API response; updated UI hint in incident_viewer.js to surface the reason. See commit 8c7b412 and web change.

Priya Natarajan: Can we ensure the default attenuation policy is conservative (95% allow for new alert types) and document the rollback steps? Also please add telemetry that counts false-suppression incidents.

Samira Alvarez: Default policy set to conservative in stepwise_template.yaml and docs include rollback + metric 'attenuator.false_suppression_count'. Added e2e that simulates false suppression and counts metric emission.
Jonah Becker: Integration tests flaky on CI once; reran and passed. Approving once green.
Jonah Becker: approved
ci_checks: integration-tests: pass (flaky on first run, rerun OK)
source.qa_notes: Local test harness included (e2e/attenuator_e2e_test.sh). QA should validate: (1) attenuation dry-run output matches live decision, (2) UI displays reason_code and provides an immediate 'unmute' button, (3) false_suppression_count increments on suppressed alerts that later escalate, (4) playbook steps created by compose-playbook contain conditional telemetry checks and a top-level priority score.
source.rolling_strategy: Feature gated by flag 'feature.attenuator_v1'. Default off for orgs with < 24h paging SLA. We rollout in three phases: dev, 10% canary, org-wide. Canary can be disabled via admin console or oncallctl attenuate --disable-canary.
source.migration_notes: No DB migrations. New metrics (attenuator.*) and small schema add to incident metadata: 'suppression' block added to alert documents. Backwards-compatible: absent block => treated as not suppressed.
source.docs_changed: Added operational guide docs/oncall/attenuator.md with rationale, attenuation heuristics, safe rollback, metrics to monitor, and recommended playbook templates. Also added CLI usage examples and troubleshooting.
source.ui_integration: incident_viewer now surfaces suppression banners with reason_code and 'unmute' action; hooks into oncallctl via web socket for immediate unmute requests.
source.security_considerations: Attenuator runs within existing incident-bot service boundary and honors RBAC. 'unmute' is an action gated by oncall role. No external network calls introduced besides snapshot composer which calls existing telemetry ingest endpoint.
source.release_notes: Introduce adaptive signal attenuator (opt-in) to reduce low-signal paging noise and a stepwise playbook generator to create prioritized remediation steps. CLI: oncallctl attenuate, oncallctl compose-playbook. Feature-gated and rolled out canary-first.
source.post_merge_tasks: Monitor attenuator.false_suppression_count and alert if > 0.5% over baseline for 2h. Add a dashboard tile in SRE views to track suppression rate, allowed_rate, and median time-to-action on suppressed incidents.
source.notes: This PR was requested as part of SRE noise-reduction initiative in ENG-7821. The design prioritizes explainability for responders and explicit quick-unmute controls to avoid blind suppression. Consider follow-up work: ML-based classification model for suppression instead of heuristics, and a 'learned' policy that adapts thresholds per service over time.

source.title: Real-time Runbook Compiler and Telemetry Stitcher
source.author: Maya Chen
source.release_notes: Adds a runbook compiler service and telemetry stitcher to produce compact, sanitized incident runbooks; introduces an ephemeral debug proxy for safe live-replay and rollback suggestion flows; no user-facing breaking changes.
source.description:
Context and motivation: On-call responders frequently have to assemble fragmented signals (alerts, traces, logs, SLO deltas) into an actionable runbook under time pressure. This PR introduces a Runbook Compiler service that composes a compact, prioritized runbook from a set of incident signals and a Telemetry Stitcher that pulls and correlates relevant telemetry slices across services. The goal is to reduce TTR and make playbook suggestions reproducible and auditable.

What this PR does (summary):
- Add runbook-compiler microservice (go) that accepts an incident payload and returns a prioritized runbook (steps + rationale + safety gates).
- Add telemetry-stitcher worker (python) that builds correlated telemetry slices (spans, top traces, key logs, metric cutouts) and applies automatic sanitization rules to strip PII/keys.
- Add ephemeral-debug-proxy (node) that can safely replay a limited subset of traffic against replica services in a sandboxed environment. The proxy enforces resource/time budgets and kill switches.
- Integrations with slo-toolkit sampler: sample sets are used to pick representative traces for evaluation and to detect regressions compared with baseline runs.
- Console + incident-bot UI hooks: new /runbook-compile command and a RunbookDraft view in incident context.

Design notes and safety:
- Sanitization: telemetry-stitcher runs a tag-based sanitizer followed by deterministic hashing for identifiers. We maintain a whitelist/blacklist config stored in vault. Changes to sanitization config require operator approval.
- Access control: runbook compilation is gated to oncall+incident-bot roles; ephemeral proxy ACLs are short-lived, minted per-session via the existing token broker.
- Resource constraints: debug proxy and stitcher are constrained by CPU/memory/QPS budgets and will fallback to a degraded mode that returns pointers to raw telemetry if budgets are exhausted.

Commits (high-level):
- 1a2f3d4 add runbook-compiler service + core compilation pipeline
- 2b3e4f5 implement telemetry-stitcher and sanitization rules
- 3c4d5e6 ephemeral-debug-proxy + sandbox harness with safe rollback hooks
- 4d5e6f7 ui: incident-bot /runbook-compile command and RunbookDraft renderer
- 5e6f7g8 tests: unit + integration + sanitize regression tests
- 6f7g8h9 docs: operator guide and console help text

Files/areas touched (high level):
- services/runbook-compiler/* (new service)
- services/telemetry-stitcher/* (new worker)
- services/ephemeral-debug-proxy/* (new sandbox proxy)
- web/incident-bot/commands/runbookCompile.ts (command wiring)
- web/console/components/RunbookDraft.vue (UI)
- infra/k8s/runbook-compiler-chart.yaml (helm chart)
- infra/policies/sanitizer-config.yaml (default sanitizer config)
- test/integration/runbook_compiler_smoke_test.go

Testing and CI:
- Unit tests for compilation heuristics and sanitizer rules (coverage: ~86%).
- Integration smoke: runbook compile end-to-end against a local stitcher and a mocked telemetry backend. One intermittent e2e flake (related to external trace service timeout) was retried and passed; we added a retry wrapper to the e2e harness.
- Load test: a small synthetic load harness that validates that the compiler can handle 40 RPS with default batching and falls back gracefully when quota exceeded.

Review conversation summary:
- Priya: Requested clearer failure semantics for the compiler when telemetry is partially available. Action: added a 'degraded' runbook mode and explicit indicators in the output.
- Jonas: Asked for tighter sanitizer controls and audit logs for sanitized artifacts. Action: added audit events and a sanitizer-config change log.
- Evan: Concerned about proxy resource exhaustion. Action: added a global supervisor with circuit-breaker and a maximum replay window config.
- Follow-ups left: add a small linear ticket to instrument cost metrics for runbook compilation (ENG-8622).

Backward compatibility and rollout plan:
- Feature is behind a feature flag (runbook.compiler.enabled = false by default in prod). We will enable in staging for two weeks and run the sampler to compare generated runbooks against existing manual playbooks.
- Canary rollout via the console: 10% of incidents for the paid tier, monitor TTR and false-positive step suggestions.

Operational notes for SRE and security:
- New k8s resources live in the runbook namespace and have poddisruptionbudgets set.
- Sanitizer config is stored in vault path secret/data/incident/sanitizer. Changes to that path trigger an alert to sec-ops.
- We’ve instrumented the following metrics: runbook.compile.duration, stitcher.correlation.count, proxy.replay.count, sanitizer.redactions.count. Dashboards and alerts added in observability-pack repo (PR coming).

Why this is different: Prior PRs focused on snapshot collectors or static playbook hints. This work is the first end-to-end compiler that (1) stitches telemetry slices, (2) emits a prioritized runbook with actionable safe rollbacks, and (3) provides a controlled sandbox to validate remediation steps without touching production state.

Notes for reviewers merging: squash merge; include the merge commit message template that references ENG-8421, ENG-8593. Merged to main after a green CI and approvals from two reviewers.

If anything is unclear or you want to run the sandbox locally, see docs/operators/runbook-compiler.md and the quickstart in services/runbook-compiler/README.md.

- Load test: a small synthetic load harness that validates that the compiler can handle 40 RPS with default batching and falls back gracefully when quota exceeded.

Review conversation summary:
- Priya: Requested clearer failure semantics for the compiler when telemetry is partially available. Action: added a 'degraded' runbook mode and explicit indicators in the output.
- Jonas: Asked for tighter sanitizer controls and audit logs for sanitized artifacts. Action: added audit events and a sanitizer-config change log.
- Evan: Concerned about proxy resource exhaustion. Action: added a global supervisor with circuit-breaker and a maximum replay window config.
- Follow-ups left: add a small linear ticket to instrument cost metrics for runbook compilation (ENG-8622).

Backward compatibility and rollout plan:
- Feature is behind a feature flag (runbook.compiler.enabled = false by default in prod). We will enable in staging for two weeks and run the sampler to compare generated runbooks against existing manual playbooks.
- Canary rollout via the console: 10% of incidents for the paid tier, monitor TTR and false-positive step suggestions.

Operational notes for SRE and security:
- New k8s resources live in the runbook namespace and have poddisruptionbudgets set.
- Sanitizer config is stored in vault path secret/data/incident/sanitizer. Changes to that path trigger an alert to sec-ops.
- We’ve instrumented the following metrics: runbook.compile.duration, stitcher.correlation.count, proxy.replay.count, sanitizer.redactions.count. Dashboards and alerts added in observability-pack repo (PR coming).

Why this is different: Prior PRs focused on snapshot collectors or static playbook hints. This work is the first end-to-end compiler that (1) stitches telemetry slices, (2) emits a prioritized runbook with actionable safe rollbacks, and (3) provides a controlled sandbox to validate remediation steps without touching production state.

Notes for reviewers merging: squash merge; include the merge commit message template that references ENG-8421, ENG-8593. Merged to main after a green CI and approvals from two reviewers.

If anything is unclear or you want to run the sandbox locally, see docs/operators/runbook-compiler.md and the quickstart in services/runbook-compiler/README.md.

source.title: runbook-telemetry-heuristics-and-blindspot-prober
source.author: Maya Chen
source.description:
Problem: our runbook suggestion flow surface-level checks (restarts, scaling, config flags) but frequently misses gaps in telemetry — 'blindspots' where a missing or mis-tagged metric/tracing span causes responders to chase symptoms. This PR adds a lightweight heuristics engine that analyzes incoming incident context + historical telemetry expectations and surfaces likely blindspots, and a safe 'blindspot prober' that can run read-only checks against telemetry pipelines to validate assumptions.

What this does (high level):
- Adds a telemetry heuristics module to incident-bot that computes a blindspot_score for an incident based on absent expected signals (metrics/traces/log markers) and signal-quality indicators (sample rates, tag variance, TTL gaps).
- Introduces a blindspot prober that runs ephemeral, read-only queries (metrics histogram checks, span-presence checks, and tag-resolution sanity probes) against configured telemetry backends (Prometheus/Graphite, Jaeger/Tempo, and S3/Log-aggregation). The prober is gated and runs with sampling & rate limits.
- Enhances runbook suggestion generation: when blindspot_score > threshold, incident-bot injects concrete probe steps into the suggested runbook (queries to run, tags to check, quick remediation stubs).
- Adds feature flagging (incident_bot.features.blindspot_detection) and a per-org sampling config (orgConfig.blindspotSamplingPct) to control rollout.
- Telemetry: emits new metrics incident_bot.blindspot_score and incident_bot.blindspot_probes_run with labels (org, backend, result).

Design notes and safety:
- Prober is read-only and only executes queries that are explicitly marked safe in the backend config. We enforce a strict query whitelist per-backend; arbitrary queries are forbidden.
- Sampling default is 2% across all free-tier orgs and 10% for verified customers; can be tuned via org config.
- To avoid noisy oncall behavior we produce a separate 'blindspot advisory' card and do NOT auto-trigger remediation.

Implementation summary (commits):
- commit 1: add telemetry heuristics engine and blindspot scoring prototype (internal API + tests)
- commit 2: implement blindspot prober, backend adapters for Prometheus and Jaeger, config knobs, and telemetry metrics
- commit 3: runbook suggestion integration, CLI flags, docs, and unit/integration tests

Changed files (high level):
- pkg/heuristics/blindspot.go — new scoring engine (core algorithm with configurable signal expectations and TTL checks).
- pkg/prober/prober.go — orchestrates safe probes, sampling, and result normalization.
- pkg/backends/prometheus_adapter.go — safe query wrapper and whitelist enforcement.
- pkg/backends/jaeger_adapter.go — cheap span-presence checks and tag-resolve helper.
- cmd/incident-bot/flags.go — new feature flags and runtime knobs.
- webui/runbooks/suggestions_renderer.go — inject advisory cards and probe steps.
- docs/operational/runbooks.md — documentation and rollout checklist.

Small illustrative snippet added to blindspot.go to document expected signal config (kept short in PR):

// expectedSignals: map[service] = ["http_request_total", "db_query_latency_ms"]
// TTL indicates how long we expect a signal to be present before flagging a gap

Review conversation (summarized; chronological):
- Alexei Petrov (reviewer): "Nice approach. Two concerns: 1) sampling defaults — 2% seems low for signal coverage on small teams; can we make it org-configurable and increase for verified customers? 2) whitelist enforcement: how do we prevent ops from adding expensive queries?"
- Maya Chen (author): "Answered inline: sampling is org-configurable (see orgConfig.blindspotSamplingPct) and defaults are conservative; updated docs and added verified-customer override. For whitelist, adapters require an admin-level 'safe_query' entry and queries are introspected for their selector type (histogram/counter only); arbitrary PromQL with subqueries is disallowed. Added tests for blacklist attempts.")
- Priya Raman (reviewer): "Security review request: ensure credentials used by the prober are scoped read-only and rotated. Also request an opt-out for orgs that do not permit telemetry queries."
- Maya Chen: "Added README section on credential scoping and a per-org disable switch (orgConfig.disableBlindspotProbes). Also added RBAC check in prober bootstrap.")
- Jordan Blake (approver): "Looks good; tests are solid. Two small nits on naming and a log-level change. Approved after nits fixed."

CI checks (timeline):
- unit-tests — pass
- integration-tests (telemetry adapters mocked) — pass
- lint — pass (fixed a couple of style issues in prober.go)
- e2e — flaky initially (timeout in Jaeger adapter test), reran and passed

Merge outcome: merged on 2026-03-02 via squash merge. Release notes appended. Feature flag disabled by default; rollout plan in docs/operational/runbooks.md.

Release notes (for changelog):
- incident-bot: add blindspot detection and safe telemetry prober to surface missing telemetry signals and augment runbooks with concrete probe steps. Feature gated and off by default.

Testing and QA notes:
- Added unit tests for scoring thresholds, prober sampling, and backend whitelist enforcement.
- Added integration tests that spin up lightweight mock Prometheus/Jaeger endpoints; e2e smoke test included in CI.
- QA run requested by Priya; sign-off included in review thread.
source.commits_summary: 3 commits — heuristics engine (prototype and tests); prober + backend adapters + telemetry; runbook integration + docs + flags
source.changed_files: pkg/heuristics/blindspot.go, pkg/prober/prober.go, pkg/backends/prometheus_adapter.go, pkg/backends/jaeger_adapter.go, cmd/incident-bot/flags.go, webui/runbooks/suggestions_renderer.go, docs/operational/runbooks.md
source.review_thread: Alexei: asked about sampling defaults and whitelist enforcement. Maya: added orgConfig.blindspotSamplingPct and whitelist tests. Priya: requested security review and opt-out; Maya: added orgConfig.disableBlindspotProbes and RBAC checks. Jordan: approved after small naming/log nits fixed.
source.ci_checks: unit-tests: pass; integration-tests: pass; lint: pass; e2e: pass (rerun after Jaeger timeout)
source.release_notes:
- New: blindspot detection for runbooks (feature flagged).
- New: safe, read-only telemetry prober integrates with Prometheus and Jaeger.
- Config: orgConfig.blindspotSamplingPct, orgConfig.disableBlindspotProbes, incident_bot.features.blindspot_detection
source.notes_for_ops: Feature is off by default. Enable via feature flag and set orgConfig.blindspotSamplingPct to desired sampling. Ensure prober credentials are read-only and safe_query whitelist is populated before enabling. Roll out gradually and monitor incident_bot.blindspot_probes_run and incident_bot.blindspot_score metrics to observe false positives.
source.help_wanted: We could use a follow-up to add a Tempo/OTel adapter and to instrument a short dashboard for blindspot score distributions by org. Volunteers: pairing with Observability team recommended.

source.title: Contextual Hypothesis Writer and Sandboxed Fix Prover
source.author: Aisha Kapoor
source.description:
Context and motivation:
This PR adds two coordinated features to the incident-bot suite: (1) a Contextual Hypothesis Writer that synthesizes prioritized remediation hypotheses from alert telemetry and runbook fragments, and (2) a Sandboxed Fix Prover that attempts safe, read-only or reversible dry-run fixes in ephemeral sandboxes to validate hypotheses before recommending operator action. The goal is to reduce mean time to mitigation for operational incidents while keeping all experiments isolated and auditable.

High-level design and behavior:
- Hypothesis Writer consumes the incident snapshot (logs, traces, metrics, recent deploys, route maps) and runbook snippets to propose 2-5 ranked hypotheses with rationale and confidence score. It attaches minimal reproduction hints and a short sequence of commands or API calls required to validate the hypothesis.
- Fix Prover executes validation workflows in ephemeral sandboxes with strict resource/time limits and capability restrictions. Each prover run produces a non-destructive evidence bundle: commands executed, API call traces, snapshots of pre/post read-only state where applicable, and a safety verdict (validated / not validated / inconclusive).
- Communication model: incident-bot UI shows hypotheses inline with a "Validate in Sandbox" action. Sandbox runs are gated behind RBAC; only users with the prover:run role can trigger them. All sandbox runs are recorded and retained for audit.

Commits summary:
- 8f3a2d5: initial hypothesis writer core + ranking model glue (feature flags for rollout)
- bc91e4b: add runbook fragment linker and heuristics for metric-to-action mapping
- 2cd9f44: sandboxed prover framework (k8s ephemeral job runner + policy engine)
- f7b0a77: prover safety policies (capability whitelists, syscall filter shim, timeouts)
- 9a2b1e3: UI wiring: validate button, status flow, and audit view
- d4a0c3f: tests (unit + sandbox integration tests) and docs

Files / areas changed (high-level):
- cmd/incident-bot/handlers/hypothesis_writer.go (new)
- internal/sandbox/prover/* (new framework + policy configs)
- ui/components/HypothesisCard.tsx (UI hooks and button)
- integrations/k8s/ephemeral_runner.yaml (deployment template with resource limits)
- docs/oncall/hypothesis-generator.md (new operator docs)
- test/e2e/sandbox_prover_test.go (integration coverage)

Security and safety considerations addressed:
- Prover runs are strictly read-only by default; write-capable checkers require explicit feature flag and PM sign-off.
- All external command execution goes through a syscall-filtering shim + seccomp profile; network egress is restricted to internal API endpoints only.
- RBAC enforced at API layer and audited to the events timeline.
- Sensitive fields in evidence bundles are redacted by a sanitizer before storage. Redaction rules are configurable.

Testing and CI notes:
- Initial CI run had a failing integration test due to intermittent namespace cleanup; added retry+cleanup in test harness (see commit d4a0c3f).
- Security static analysis flagged uses of temporary file creation in prover; updated to use atomic temp APIs and cleared file handles on exit.
- Final CI matrix: unit (pass), linter (pass), e2e sandbox (pass after test harness fix).

Review conversation (summary):
- Owen Park: "Great direction — please show the RBAC surface and how this will look to SOC. Also add audit retention knobs."
  - Author: "Added RBAC docs, retention knobs default to 90d, made retention configurable in operator config (docs updated)."
- Lena Ho: "Sandboxing looks reasonable, but we need explicit proof that no credentials can be leaked via environment variables."
  - Author: "Added environment scrubbing and an explicit test case that injects faux credentials and asserts they are not present in evidence bundles."
- Marcelo D'Souza (security): "Rejecting any run that would result in outbound access to external internet domains; please add egress policy."
  - Author: "Implemented network policy to restrict egress to internal clusters only and added a policy test (k8s network policy)."
- Priya Natarajan: "Integration tests flaked once — please ensure cleanup does not leave stale namespaces."
  - Author: "Fixed with retries and deterministic namespace naming; added a post-test sweep job in CI."

Checklist performed before merge:
- [x] Unit tests for hypothesis ranking heuristics
- [x] Integration tests for prover sandbox lifecycle
- [x] Security review (Marcelo) and RBAC mapping
- [x] Documentation for on-call usage and opt-in rollout steps
- [x] Audit logging and retention config added
- [x] Feature flag gating (incidents.enableHypothesisWriter, incidents.enableProver)

Rollout plan:
- Step 1 (canary, 2% of tenants): enable Hypothesis Writer only (no prover) to gather feedback on hypothesis quality; monitor precision/false positives via eval prompts.
- Step 2 (internal org only): enable prover for engineers in dev orgs to exercise sandboxes.
- Step 3 (gradual): open to SRE teams once sandbox metrics (latency, resource usage, safety verdict rates) are stable.

Release notes:
- Adds a Contextual Hypothesis Writer that proposes ranked remediation hypotheses for incidents and a Sandboxed Fix Prover that validates hypotheses in ephemeral, auditable sandboxes. Feature flagged and gated by RBAC.

Merge details:
- Merged via squash on 2026-02-25 after all reviewers approved. CI green on final run.

Follow-ups / TODOs:
- Add cost accounting hooks for sandbox runs to show token/resource cost attribution (ENG-4838).
- Add a configurable approval flow for write-capable provers behind an enterprise compliance toggle.
- Improve hypothesis ranking model with additional signal sources (deploy diffs, config drift).

bc91e4b runbook fragment linker and heuristics
2cd9f44 sandboxed prover framework and job runner
f7b0a77 prover safety policies and seccomp shim
9a2b1e3 UI wiring for validate button and audit view
d4a0c3f tests and docs

Aisha Kapoor: Added RBAC docs and retention knobs (commit d4a0c3f).
Lena Ho: Add env-var scrubbing test.
Aisha Kapoor: Added sanitizer + test (commit f7b0a77).
Marcelo D'Souza: Block any external egress from provers.
Aisha Kapoor: Implemented network policy and test (commit 2cd9f44).
Priya Natarajan: Flaky test observed; please fix cleanup.
Aisha Kapoor: Fixed with deterministic namespaces and sweeper job (commit d4a0c3f).

2026-02-17: rerun CI after harness fix — unit/lint/pass, e2e/pass
source.release_notes: Adds a Contextual Hypothesis Writer and Sandboxed Fix Prover to incident-bot. Both features are behind feature flags and RBAC. Sandboxed runs are auditable, read-only by default, and enforce strict egress/capability policies.
source.notes_for_ops: Operators: enable incidents.enableHypothesisWriter=false by default. To test, follow docs/oncall/hypothesis-generator.md. Sandbox prover requires prover:run role and cluster network policy support. Monitor prover.sandbox.run_duration and prover.sandbox.safety_verdict_rate in the incidents namespace.
source.safety_and_compliance: Evidence bundles are redacted; retention default 90d. Prover runs audited and subject to RBAC. No external internet egress allowed. Seccomp profiles and network policies enforced.
source.next_actions: Track ENG-4838 for cost attribution; schedule enterprise compliance review for write-capable provers.
source.human_readable_summary: Introduce a hypothesis generator plus safe sandbox validator to let on-call engineers validate remediation steps before taking action; feature gated and audited.

- Final CI matrix: unit (pass), linter (pass), e2e sandbox (pass after test harness fix).

Review conversation (summary):
- Owen Park: "Great direction — please show the RBAC surface and how this will look to SOC. Also add audit retention knobs."
  - Author: "Added RBAC docs, retention knobs default to 90d, made retention configurable in operator config (docs updated)."
- Lena Ho: "Sandboxing looks reasonable, but we need explicit proof that no credentials can be leaked via environment variables."
  - Author: "Added environment scrubbing and an explicit test case that injects faux credentials and asserts they are not present in evidence bundles."
- Marcelo D'Souza (security): "Rejecting any run that would result in outbound access to external internet domains; please add egress policy."
  - Author: "Implemented network policy to restrict egress to internal clusters only and added a policy test (k8s network policy)."
- Priya Natarajan: "Integration tests flaked once — please ensure cleanup does not leave stale namespaces."
  - Author: "Fixed with retries and deterministic namespace naming; added a post-test sweep job in CI."

Checklist performed before merge:
- [x] Unit tests for hypothesis ranking heuristics
- [x] Integration tests for prover sandbox lifecycle
- [x] Security review (Marcelo) and RBAC mapping
- [x] Documentation for on-call usage and opt-in rollout steps
- [x] Audit logging and retention config added
- [x] Feature flag gating (incidents.enableHypothesisWriter, incidents.enableProver)

Rollout plan:
- Step 1 (canary, 2% of tenants): enable Hypothesis Writer only (no prover) to gather feedback on hypothesis quality; monitor precision/false positives via eval prompts.
- Step 2 (internal org only): enable prover for engineers in dev orgs to exercise sandboxes.
- Step 3 (gradual): open to SRE teams once sandbox metrics (latency, resource usage, safety verdict rates) are stable.

Release notes:
- Adds a Contextual Hypothesis Writer that proposes ranked remediation hypotheses for incidents and a Sandboxed Fix Prover that validates hypotheses in ephemeral, auditable sandboxes. Feature flagged and gated by RBAC.

Merge details:
- Merged via squash on 2026-02-25 after all reviewers approved. CI green on final run.

Follow-ups / TODOs:
- Add cost accounting hooks for sandbox runs to show token/resource cost attribution (ENG-4838).
- Add a configurable approval flow for write-capable provers behind an enterprise compliance toggle.
- Improve hypothesis ranking model with additional signal sources (deploy diffs, config drift).
text:
Context and motivation:
This PR adds two coordinated features to the incident-bot suite: (1) a Contextual Hypothesis Writer that synthesizes prioritized remediation hypotheses from alert telemetry and runbook fragments, and (2) a Sandboxed Fix Prover that attempts safe, read-only or reversible dry-run fixes in ephemeral sandboxes to validate hypotheses before recommending operator action. The goal is to reduce mean time to mitigation for operational incidents while keeping all experiments isolated and auditable.

High-level design and behavior:
- Hypothesis Writer consumes the incident snapshot (logs, traces, metrics, recent deploys, route maps) and runbook snippets to propose 2-5 ranked hypotheses with rationale and confidence score. It attaches minimal reproduction hints and a short sequence of commands or API calls required to validate the hypothesis.
- Fix Prover executes validation workflows in ephemeral sandboxes with strict resource/time limits and capability restrictions. Each prover run produces a non-destructive evidence bundle: commands executed, API call traces, snapshots of pre/post read-only state where applicable, and a safety verdict (validated / not validated / inconclusive).
- Communication model: incident-bot UI shows hypotheses inline with a "Validate in Sandbox" action. Sandbox runs are gated behind RBAC; only users with the prover:run role can trigger them. All sandbox runs are recorded and retained for audit.

Commits summary:
- 8f3a2d5: initial hypothesis writer core + ranking model glue (feature flags for rollout)
- bc91e4b: add runbook fragment linker and heuristics for metric-to-action mapping
- 2cd9f44: sandboxed prover framework (k8s ephemeral job runner + policy engine)
- f7b0a77: prover safety policies (capability whitelists, syscall filter shim, timeouts)
- 9a2b1e3: UI wiring: validate button, status flow, and audit view
- d4a0c3f: tests (unit + sandbox integration tests) and docs

Files / areas changed (high-level):
- cmd/incident-bot/handlers/hypothesis_writer.go (new)
- internal/sandbox/prover/* (new framework + policy configs)
- ui/components/HypothesisCard.tsx (UI hooks and button)
- integrations/k8s/ephemeral_runner.yaml (deployment template with resource limits)
- docs/oncall/hypothesis-generator.md (new operator docs)
- test/e2e/sandbox_prover_test.go (integration coverage)

Security and safety considerations addressed:
- Prover runs are strictly read-only by default; write-capable checkers require explicit feature flag and PM sign-off.
- All external command execution goes through a syscall-filtering shim + seccomp profile; network egress is restricted to internal API endpoints only.
- RBAC enforced at API layer and audited to the events timeline.
- Sensitive fields in evidence bundles are redacted by a sanitizer before storage. Redaction rules are configurable.

Testing and CI notes:
- Initial CI run had a failing integration test due to intermittent namespace cleanup; added retry+cleanup in test harness (see commit d4a0c3f).
- Security static analysis flagged uses of temporary file creation in prover; updated to use atomic temp APIs and cleared file handles on exit.
- Final CI matrix: unit (pass), linter (pass), e2e sandbox (pass after test harness fix).

Review conversation (summary):
- Owen Park: "Great direction — please show the RBAC surface and how this will look to SOC. Also add audit retention knobs."
  - Author: "Added RBAC docs, retention knobs default to 90d, made retention configurable in operator config (docs updated)."
- Lena Ho: "Sandboxing looks reasonable, but we need explicit proof that no credentials can be leaked via environment variables."
  - Author: "Added environment scrubbing and an explicit test case that injects faux credentials and asserts they are not present in evidence bundles."
- Marcelo D'Souza (security): "Rejecting any run that would result in outbound access to external internet domains; please add egress policy."
  - Author: "Implemented network policy to restrict egress to internal clusters only and added a policy test (k8s network policy)."
- Priya Natarajan: "Integration tests flaked once — please ensure cleanup does not leave stale namespaces."
  - Author: "Fixed with retries and deterministic namespace naming; added a post-test sweep job in CI."

- [x] Unit tests for hypothesis ranking heuristics
- [x] Integration tests for prover sandbox lifecycle
- [x] Security review (Marcelo) and RBAC mapping
- [x] Documentation for on-call usage and opt-in rollout steps
- [x] Audit logging and retention config added
- [x] Feature flag gating (incidents.enableHypothesisWriter, incidents.enableProver)

Rollout plan:
- Step 1 (canary, 2% of tenants): enable Hypothesis Writer only (no prover) to gather feedback on hypothesis quality; monitor precision/false positives via eval prompts.
- Step 2 (internal org only): enable prover for engineers in dev orgs to exercise sandboxes.
- Step 3 (gradual): open to SRE teams once sandbox metrics (latency, resource usage, safety verdict rates) are stable.

source.title: Sampled probe choreographer and on-call handoff scorecard
source.author: Asha Menon
source.description: Motivation: Reduce noisy probes and pager fatigue while improving first-responder context during handoffs by sampling probes intelligently and producing a compact handoff scorecard. This PR implements a sampled probe choreographer that schedules a small, representative set of lightweight probes per on-call window, tracks probe provenance, and emits a one-line handoff scorecard summarizing signal health and probe coverage. Changes include: 1) a new choreographer component (choreographer/sampled_scheduler.py) that picks probes deterministically using a weighted reservoir sampler tied to on-call rotation ids; 2) probe metadata propagation to the incident snapshot (snapshot/attachments.md -> snapshot metadata additions) so responders can see sample reason and TTL; 3) a handoff scorecard generator (handoff/scorecard.py) that produces a compact human-friendly summary and machine-readable tags for rollups; 4) a lightweight CLI to preview scheduled probes for the upcoming shift (bin/preview-probes) and a console widget to surface the scorecard in the on-call dashboard. Safety/perf: the choreographer enforces a configurable probe budget per rotation (default 8 probes per 6h window), backoff for noisy endpoints (exponential backoff on repeated failures), and samples across metadata dimensions (region, service, metric-family) to preserve coverage. Tests and CI: unit tests for sampler, deterministic-seeding tests, e2e simulated on-call run that verifies scorecard appears in handoff payloads. Rollout plan: feature gated behind incident_features.sampled_probes; gradual enablement by rotation -> team -> org with metrics monitored (probe hit rate, responder opens, pager volume). Checklist: - [x] design doc linked: https://linear.app/ENG/notes/sampled-probe-choreographer - [x] unit + integration tests - [x] add feature flag - [x] update docs and console widget - [x] added metrics/traces for rollout. Commits summary: "init sampled choreographer and basic scheduler"; "propagate probe metadata to snapshots"; "handoff scorecard formatting and tags"; "cli preview and console widget wiring"; "tests and docs". Changed files (high level): choreographer/sampled_scheduler.py, handoff/scorecard.py, snapshot/metadata_adapter.py, bin/preview-probes, web/ui/widgets/handoff_scorecard.vue, tests/test_sampler.py, docs/oncall/sampled-probes.md. Small illustrative snippet included in scheduler (single-line): "selection = reservoir.sample(candidates, budget, seed=rotation_id_hash)". Review convo summary: Ben Carter: "Can we ensure sampling is stable across rotation reassignments? If rotation id changes we'll lose continuity." Author reply: "Yes — scheduler seeds with rotation_id_hash derived from rotation uid and start timestamp; added deterministic-seeding tests (see test_sampler.py)." Maya Patel (requested changes): "Need clearer observability: add histogram for probe latency and a tag for sample_reason in traces." Author reply: "Added histograms and propagated sample_reason tag into trace spans; updated trace schema doc." Liam O'Connor: "Approve with minor nit on CLI help wording." Author reply: "Addressed nit and re-ran CI." CI: pipeline passed (unit+lint+integration); one flaky e2e rerun was required and passed on rerun. Merge outcome: merged by squash on 2026-02-25 (merge method: squash, merge commit message: "Sampled probe choreographer + handoff scorecard (ENG-3112)"). Release notes: "Added experimental sampled probe scheduling to reduce pager noise and a compact handoff scorecard that surfaces probe coverage and signal health. Feature gated and off by default." Additional notes: Monitoring dashboards created under SLO/Oncall (probe_budget_utilization, handoff_score_distribution). Follow-ups: instrument probe deduplication across overlapping rotations (ENG-3120), expand sampling policy to use anomaly-weighted scoring (SRE-47).
commits: init sampled choreographer and basic scheduler
propagate probe metadata to snapshots
handoff scorecard formatting and tags
cli preview and console widget wiring
tests and docs
review_comments: Ben Carter: Can we ensure sampling is stable across rotation reassignments?
Asha Menon: Scheduler seeds with rotation_id_hash; added tests.
Maya Patel: Add histogram for probe latency and sample_reason tag in traces.
Asha Menon: Added histograms and propagated sample_reason tag.
Liam O'Connor: minor nit on CLI help wording.
Asha Menon: Wording fixed and CI re-run.
source.release_notes: Experimental: sampled probe scheduling to reduce noisy probes and a compact handoff scorecard; feature gated (incident_features.sampled_probes=false by default). Monitor probe_budget_utilization and handoff_score_distribution dashboards.
source.notes_for_sre: Watch for drop in overall probe traffic and increase in per-probe importance; rollback if responder open rate drops unexpectedly. Follow-up tickets: ENG-3120 (dedupe), SRE-47 (anomaly-weighted sampling).

source.title: Enrich on-call diagnostics with automated runbook suggestions and triage helpers
source.author: Ava Chen
source.description: Context: On-call teams often waste critical minutes assembling the same diagnostic surface (pod logs, recent deploys, graph snapshots, SLO deltas) before they can follow runbooks. This PR adds an automated diagnostics gatherer plus a lightweight runbook-suggestion engine to incident-bot so on-call responders get an actionable triage checklist and templated next steps in Slack/PagerDuty threads. Motivation: reduce mean time to acknowledge (MTTA) and mean time to resolution (MTTR) by surfacing high-value signals automatically and recommending runbook steps tailored to the observed signals. Summary of changes: 1) diagnostics worker: new background worker that gathers a bounded set of artifacts (pod logs tails, pod event summaries, recent deploy metadata, SLO deltas from slo-toolkit, topology hints) with configurable TTL and size limits; 2) suggestion engine: rule-based scorer + template library that maps diagnostic signatures to ranked runbook suggestions and immediate mitigation commands; 3) on-call commands: /incident-diagnose which triggers a diagnostics snapshot and posts a suggested runbook checklist back into Slack; 4) triage UI: compact summary card and links to full artifacts (pre-signed URLs) plus confidence score; 5) observability and audit: metrics for diagnoses-per-hour, avg suggestion confidence, and feature flags to enable/disable production use. Compatibility: integrates with existing Slack and PagerDuty hooks, and fetches SLOs using the slo-toolkit API. Security: diagnostics snapshots include redaction hooks and respect KMS-backed secrets; we add an opt-out setting per team. Rollout: feature-gated behind the config flag incident_bot.autorunbook.enabled (default off). Rollout plan includes staged enablement for internal SRE team, canary on a small Slack workspace, and full rollout after 2 weeks of monitoring. Checklist: - [x] Unit tests for scorer heuristics - [x] Integration tests against a local slo-toolkit stub - [x] e2e test that posts to a test Slack workspace - [x] Docs + runbook templates added - [x] Feature flag covered and telemetry emitted Testing notes: use the provided test harness under test/harness/diagnostics to simulate cluster events and SLO regressions. See linear ENG-942 for acceptance criteria and the linked design doc (internal link) for decision rationale. Backwards compatibility: when the feature flag is off, no runtime change; the commands are hidden. Rollback: disable feature flag and revoke pre-signed artifact URLs if necessary.
commits: a1b2c3: add diagnostics worker and scaffold (diag/collector)
d4e5f6: implement rule-based suggestion engine and templates (diag/suggester)
f7a8b9: slack command /incident-diagnose and HTTP handlers
c0d1e2: integration tests and e2e harness for slo-toolkit
b3c4d5: docs and runbook template examples, add metrics and feature flag
review_comments: Diego Ramos: nice end-to-end design; please add a regression unit test for the weighing fallback when multiple rules match
Ava Chen (author): added fallback unit and updated scorer comments in last commit
Mina Patel: can we limit the pre-signed URL TTL to 24h by default? Risk of stale artifacts surfaced in channel
Ava Chen: default TTL set to 24h; added per-team override and documented decision
Samir Kapoor: small nit - use get_logger('incident') instead of root logger; also add a metric for suggestion_confidence distribution
Ava Chen: switched to incident logger and added histogram metric for suggestion_confidence
ci_checks: deploy-preview: created (no changes to infra except diag-worker manifest)
source.merge_outcome: merged (squash) by Mina Patel using merge-squash; commit message updated to include release notes
source.release_notes: Add automated on-call diagnostics and runbook suggestion engine for incident-bot. Feature is behind a feature flag (incident_bot.autorunbook.enabled). Enables /incident-diagnose command to produce a ranked list of suggested runbook steps and links to collected artifacts. Default disabled; internal rollout first.
source.risk_assessment: Medium. New background worker will increase API calls to slo-toolkit and create temporary artifact storage; mitigations include rate limits, feature flag, and TTLs. Redaction pipeline lowers risk of secret leaks.
source.deploy_instructions: Apply k8s manifest deploy/k8s/diag-worker-deployment.yaml to a canary namespace first. Enable incident_bot.autorunbook.enabled for the internal SRE workspace via configs/feature_flags.yml. Monitor metrics: diag_snapshots_total, suggestion_confidence_histogram, and slack_command_error_rate. Revert by setting flag to false and removing canary manifest.
suggested_followups: SRE-315: Create cross-team runbook library and mapping contributor guide
OPS-420: Add retention policy automation for stored artifacts

source.title: Post rollout auto-rollback events to Slack (concise notification + links)
source.author: Rafael Mendes
source.body: Summary: incident-bot now posts a Slack notification when the rollout controller triggers an automatic safety action due to an SLO breach (auto_rollback, auto_pause, or rollback_suppressed). The message is intentionally short, includes the rollout_id and scope, and provides deep links to (1) the rollout in Console, (2) the rollout SLO dashboard, and (3) the audit event details. Motivation: during the 2026-02 simulations and recent customer escalations, oncall often learned about auto-mitigations only after secondary alerts or customer reports; this closes the loop and reduces time-to-context while keeping noise bounded. Event source: consumes the new structured audit event emitted to the event stream (redwood PR-28435) with event_type=rollout.auto_action.*. The handler maps event subtypes to Slack severity and routing without paging by default (notify-only) to avoid paging storms. Message format (example): "[AUTO-ROLLBACK] rollout=rol_01HT… scope=route:/v1/chat model=llama3.1-70b-v3 region=us-east tier=dedicated reason=p99_latency_burnrate_fast" followed by links: Console rollout, Grafana dashboard, Audit event (internal). Guardrails: (a) dedupe by (rollout_id, action, step_id) for 10 minutes, (b) drop messages if required links can’t be constructed (still increments a metric), (c) redacts customer identifiers (uses customer_tier + optional org_id hash only), (d) rate limits per channel to 1 msg/sec with burst=5. Routing: auto_rollback -> #eng-oncall (notify) + #eng-sre (notify); auto_pause -> #eng-platform (notify); rollback_suppressed -> thread reply on the most recent auto action message if present, otherwise #eng-sre (notify). Configuration: adds rollout_auto_actions.enabled (default false in prod; true in staging), rollout_auto_actions.channels overrides, and a link template block for Console/Grafana base URLs per environment. Observability: emits incident_bot_slack_posts_total{type=rollout_auto_action,outcome=sent|dropped|rate_limited|deduped} and incident_bot_rollout_auto_action_events_total{action=rollback|pause|suppressed}. Testing: unit tests cover payload parsing, formatting, dedupe, and redaction; an integration test uses a fixture event to validate generated Slack blocks; updated run instructions in README. Rollout plan: staging enablement first (paired with redwood PR-28490), then prod behind flag once dashboards/alerts are confirmed (observability-pack PR-913).
commits: Add rollout auto action event handler + Slack formatter
Implement dedupe cache + per-channel rate limiting
Add config schema + env defaults (staging on, prod off)
Add metrics for send/dedupe/drop outcomes
Unit + integration tests for rollout event notifications
Docs: README update for local testing + troubleshooting
source.checks: CI: build (pass), unit tests (pass), lint (pass), integration tests (pass). Note: initial run failed due to a flaky timing assertion in dedupe TTL test; fixed by injecting clock and reran successfully.
source.conversation: Sean Gallagher: Can we ensure this doesn’t page? We got burned by incident-bot integrations turning into paging storms. Author (Rafael): Confirmed notify-only; no PagerDuty integration. Also added dedupe + per-channel rate limiting and made prod default disabled behind rollout_auto_actions.enabled. Aisha Bello: Please include Grafana links to the rollout SLO view and the ‘auto-rollback triggered’ panel so oncall can jump directly to context. Rafael: Added dashboard link template with route/model/region variables and verified in staging; falls back to generic dashboard if scope is missing. Felix Schneider: Any privacy issues with including org/customer names? We should avoid posting customer identifiers in shared channels. Rafael: Redacted org_name; only includes customer_tier and (optional) hashed org_id. Added tests to ensure no raw org_name fields leak. Logan Wright: Ensure the bot increments a metric when dropping due to missing links; otherwise we’ll silently lose notifications. Rafael: Added incident_bot_slack_posts_total{outcome=dropped,drop_reason=missing_link_template|invalid_payload}. Approvals after requested changes.
release_notes: incident-bot can now post concise Slack notifications for rollout auto actions (rollback/pause/suppressed) with deep links to Console + Grafana.
Feature is behind rollout_auto_actions.enabled (prod default: false; staging default: true).

source.title: Add bridged on-call context and automated escalation templates
source.author: Sofia Martinez
source.ci_status: pass (integration flaky on first run, rerun succeeded)
source.description: Motivation: on-call responders frequently need full incident context when incidents are bridged across multiple chat channels and paging systems; manual copy/paste is error-prone and slows TTR. This PR introduces a compact, serializable "bridged context" snapshot that travels with the incident across channels and an opinionated library of parameterized escalation templates that the bot can surface or send automatically. Changes include: 1) context capture: snapshot of current runbook pointer, top-3 alerts, recent error rates, and active mitigations; 2) context propagation: add signed metadata token to bridged messages so downstream responders receive identical context; 3) template library: YAML-driven templates with conditional rendering, throttling and dedupe keys; 4) escalation rules: configurable selectors that map alert signals + SLO breach state to template choices and escalation targets; 5) telemetry and auditing: new metrics and audit entries for every template render/send; 6) feature gating: rollout via feature flag per-org with safe defaults. Backwards compatibility: if a downstream bot doesn't understand the token, it ignores it and nothing breaks; templates are opt-in per-oncall rotation. Testing: unit and integration harness simulating bridged Slack<->PagerDuty flows, contract tests for token validity, and an e2e smoke in staging. Rollout: enable for internal SRE rotation (10% of incidents) for 48h, monitor metrics, then expand to all teams. See ENG-4021 for design notes and SUP-912 for support runbook updates. Checklist: [x] design doc linked [x] unit tests [x] integration tests [x] telemetry added [x] security review requested [x] staged rollout planned.
commits: feat(context): capture bridged channel metadata and ephemeral incident snapshot
feat(templates): add YAML-driven template library and renderer
feat(escalation): implement rule engine for automatic template selection
chore(tests): add simulated slack/pagerduty bridge scenarios
fix(security): sign context tokens with rotated HMAC key and validation
reviews: Ethan Park (requested changes): We should avoid sending full stack traces in context tokens; please confirm we sanitize sensitive fields. Author: sanitized top-level message and excluded traces; also added contract test to assert no secrets are present.

Priya Desai (comment): Template throttling looks good, but what happens when multiple rules match? Author: added deterministic priority ordering and a tiebreaker based on rotation id; documented in templates YAML schema.
Marcus Liu (approved): Verified e2e staging run, metrics look stable and no duplicate escalations observed.
CI Bot: initial integration failed on flaky external-call; author reran pipeline and all checks passed.
Security Review (comment): token signing is acceptable but rotate keys quarterly; author: added key-rotation stub and notes to ops playbook.
source.release_notes: Adds bridged on-call context propagation and automated escalation templates; feature-gated and opt-in; metrics: bridged_oncall.escalation_sent_total, bridged_oncall.duplicate_dedupe_blocked_total; see docs/runbooks/bridged-context.md for usage.
source.testing: Unit tests for snapshot and template rendering; integration tests for Slack<->PagerDuty bridging; staging smoke against a snapshot of production data; contract tests to ensure token ABI stability.
source.rollout_plan: Canary: enable for internal SRE rotation (10% incidents) for 48h -> monitor errors, duplicate escalations, and latency -> 50% teams for 72h -> GA. Feature flag per-org with emergency kill-switch.
source.risk: low-medium: risk of duplicate escalations if dedupe id generation fails; mitigations: deterministic dedupe keys, rate limits, and audit alerts; security review required before GA.
source.notes_for_oncall: When enabled, human responders will see a 'bridged context' card with runbook pointer and recent alerts; if auto-escalation triggers, the incident will include the template message and metadata; to opt-out add 'bridged_context: false' to the rotation config.

source.title: oncall handoff checksum and telemetry synthesizer for compact context transfer
source.author: Maya Chen
source.description: Motivation: handoffs during high-pressure incidents frequently lost critical context (partial traces, clipped logs, ad-hoc snippets). This PR adds two complementary features to reduce costly context gaps: 1) a deterministic handoff checksum attached to every incident-bot handoff message that verifies payload fidelity across tools; 2) a telemetry synthesizer service that constructs a compact, privacy-safe summary of missing spans/events when downstream telemetry is sparse. The checksum is a canonical JSON serialization + SHA256 digest with stable ordering and configurable salt, surfaced as 'handoff_checksum' in handoff payloads and displayed in the oncall UI; operators can use it to assert the handoff message received by downstream runbooks/Slack threads matches the sent content. The telemetry synthesizer runs as an opt-in stage during handoff: it inspects available traces/logs, infers any missing 'critical markers' (eg. deploy markers, leader-loss events, DB failovers) using lightweight heuristics, and emits a synthetic summary under a retention TTL and privacy filters (PII redaction). Implementation notes: added new CLI flags --handoff-checksum and --synth-telemetry-ttl; backend uses an in-memory LRU cache to dedupe synth outputs; synth output includes provenance metadata and confidence scores for each synthesized item. Checklist: - implement checksum generation and attach to handoff payloads - add synth heuristics and TTL-based retention - UI: show checksum and synth summary link in handoff modal - tests: unit tests for canonicalization, synth heuristics, and e2e handoff flow - docs: upgrade oncall runbook template with checksum verification steps High level commits: 1) add checksum generator and unit tests 2) add telemetry synthesizer module + heuristics 3) wire CLI flags and config parsing 4) UI changes to surface checksum and synth links 5) tests, docs, and changelog. Changed areas: backend/handshake/handshake.go (checksum logic), backend/synth/synthesizer.py (heuristics engine), cli/handoff_cli.go (flags), webapp/src/components/HandoffModal.tsx (UI), docs/oncall/handoff-checksum.md, tests/{unit,e2e}_handoff_test.*. Review conversation summary: Liam O'Connor: 'Strong utility, but ensure TTL and privacy filters are explicit and configurable.' Maya: 'Added TTL flag and explicit redaction step; default TTL set to 7 days and can be shortened per-org.' Priya Natarajan: 'Can we gate synth by incident severity? Least-privilege for low-priority incidents.' Maya: 'Added severity gating (configurable threshold) and included examples in docs.' Diego Alvarez: 'Please add load tests for the synth path.' Maya: 'Added simple load smoke test and circuit-breaker to prevent synth storms.' CI summary: initial run failed lint on webapp; fixed formatting and re-ran CI; final run passed all checks (unit, lint, e2e smoke). Merge outcome: merged via squash-merge, changelog entry added to release notes. Release notes: 'improve oncall handoffs: include deterministic checksum for handoff payloads and optional synthesized telemetry summaries to fill sparse traces; configurable TTL and severity gating; docs updated with verification steps and examples.'
source.release_notes: Added deterministic handoff checksum and optional synthesized telemetry summaries for incident handoffs. Configurable TTL, severity gating, and privacy redaction. See docs/oncall/handoff-checksum.md for verification steps.

source.title: evidence-pack-assembler-and-runbook-heuristics
source.author: Aisha Patel
source.description:
Summary: This change adds an "evidence pack" assembler to the incident-bot pipeline and a set of runbook heuristics to automatically curate the most relevant diagnostics for an alert. The assembler collects contextual artifacts (metrics snapshots, recent logs, trace spans, KV cache summaries, and SLO delta snapshots), ranks them using lightweight heuristics, prunes/pivots sensitive fields, and generates a compressed evidence bundle that can be attached to runbooks and pager notifications.

Motivation and context: On long-running incidents engineers spend a lot of time pulling the same set of data from multiple systems for triage. This PR is targeted at reducing mean time to acknowledgement and the handoff friction by producing a focused, size-bounded artifact that the oncall and responder can consume in the first 10 minutes. It also standardizes what we include for downstream postmortems and automated RCA stitches. Related tickets: ENG-4521 (runbook automation), SRE-78 (oncall evidence standard).

Design: 
- Evidence assembler service (in-process to incident-bot) with pluggable collectors for: metrics snapshots, recent logs (time-window based with token caps), traces (top N spans by error rate), config diffs, and a short KV cache dump summary.
- Heuristics engine prioritizes artifacts by signal-to-noise: anomaly score (from SLO-delta), error-class frequency, recent deploy detection, and cross-service call error propagation. We keep the heuristics intentionally interpretable (weighted rules + decay factors) to facilitate debugging and tuning.
- Privacy & size constraints: All artifacts are subject to field scrubbing rules using the existing redaction registry; large payloads are truncated or replaced with a sampling summary. Evidence packs are capped at 2MB by default and stored encrypted (KMS-tagged) for 7 days; retention and KMS key are configurable via RBACed console controls.
- Integration points: The assembler runs during alert enrichment (pre-notification), can be requested ad-hoc from the incident console, and is attached to runbook drafts created by the incident-bot. A feature flag gated rollout allows opt-in by team.

Implementation highlights: 
- new internal/curation/assembler.go: core orchestration, assembler API, size-aware bundling.
- new internal/curation/heuristics.go: compact rule engine and default rule set with unit tests.
- pkg/runbooks/generator.ts: runbook templates extended to include a signed link to evidence pack and human-readable summary.
- ops/playbooks/templates/evidence-default.yaml: configuration schema exposed to ops.
- docs/runbooks/evidence-pack.md: usage, troubleshooting, and security considerations.

Testing & CI: Unit tests for the heuristics rules and scrubber added; an e2e workflow (.github/workflows/evidence-pack.yml) runs a smoke assembly against fixture data and validates size/encryption headers. I intentionally included simulated noisy alerts to assert the assembler prunes correctly. CI passed after a single rerun where a flaky external test (trace fixture) was adjusted.

Rollout plan: staged by team flag. Phase 1: enable for SRE and a small set of platform teams (2 weeks) to capture feedback and tweak heuristics. Phase 2: broader opt-in and metrics collection (evidence pack attach rate, median size, time-to-first-artifact). Fallback: if assembler fails or triggers size/security validation, incident-bot attaches a short diagnostic summary and logs the error for postmortem.

Checklist: 
- [x] Core assembler implemented + unit tests
- [x] Heuristics rule set + tests
- [x] Redaction and size enforcement integrated
- [x] Docs + ops config added
- [x] CI e2e workflow added
- [x] Feature-flag + staged rollout plan

Risks and mitigation: The main risk is overfitting heuristics to a narrow set of incidents and leaking sensitive fields. Mitigations: conservative default rules, rigorous redaction by registry, short retention, and a configurable opt-out.

Notes for reviewers: Focus on the rule definitions in heuristics.go (they are intentionally simple) and the scrubber integration in assembler.go. I included a couple of example evidence packs under ci/test/fixture/evidence.json for reference.

If merged, follow-up work tracked under ENG-4613 (adaptive heuristics tuning) and SRE-90 (evidence retention policy extension).
commits: a1b3c9e: add evidence pack assembler scaffold and API surface
b2f4d20: implement heuristics engine and default rule set
c34d5aa: integrate redaction registry and size caps
d44e1b3: extend runbook generator to include evidence links
e55f7c6: add docs, ops config, and CI workflow
review_comments: Marco Liu: Nice direction — can you make the heuristics weights configurable from the ops template? Hardcoding will make A/B hard. Also add a short note about the expected runtime cost for the hottest alerts.

Aisha Patel: Thanks Marco — weights are now read from ops/playbooks/templates/evidence-default.yaml with sane defaults; added a comment in heuristics.go describing typical per-alert cost (median ~45ms on p95 under load).
Dana Rivers: We should be conservative about retained fields in packs. Can we ensure no PII flows, and add an allowlist/denylist for keys?
Aisha Patel: Added integration with the existing redaction registry and an explicit denylist in the ops template; docs updated with examples of redaction behavior.
Ethan Park: CI e2e flaked once due to trace fixture TTL; lgtm after rerun and small fixture change.
Dana Rivers: Approved-by: Dana Rivers
Marco Liu: Approved-by: Marco Liu
source.release_notes: Adds an evidence pack assembler and runbook heuristics to incident-bot. Generates size-bounded, redacted diagnostic bundles that are attached to runbooks and pager notifications. Feature-flagged and rolled out to SRE/platform teams initially.

It also standardizes what we include for downstream postmortems and automated RCA stitches. Related tickets: ENG-4521 (runbook automation), SRE-78 (oncall evidence standard).

Design: 
- Evidence assembler service (in-process to incident-bot) with pluggable collectors for: metrics snapshots, recent logs (time-window based with token caps), traces (top N spans by error rate), config diffs, and a short KV cache dump summary.
- Heuristics engine prioritizes artifacts by signal-to-noise: anomaly score (from SLO-delta), error-class frequency, recent deploy detection, and cross-service call error propagation. We keep the heuristics intentionally interpretable (weighted rules + decay factors) to facilitate debugging and tuning.
- Privacy & size constraints: All artifacts are subject to field scrubbing rules using the existing redaction registry; large payloads are truncated or replaced with a sampling summary. Evidence packs are capped at 2MB by default and stored encrypted (KMS-tagged) for 7 days; retention and KMS key are configurable via RBACed console controls.
- Integration points: The assembler runs during alert enrichment (pre-notification), can be requested ad-hoc from the incident console, and is attached to runbook drafts created by the incident-bot. A feature flag gated rollout allows opt-in by team.

Implementation highlights: 
- new internal/curation/assembler.go: core orchestration, assembler API, size-aware bundling.
- new internal/curation/heuristics.go: compact rule engine and default rule set with unit tests.
- pkg/runbooks/generator.ts: runbook templates extended to include a signed link to evidence pack and human-readable summary.
- ops/playbooks/templates/evidence-default.yaml: configuration schema exposed to ops.
- docs/runbooks/evidence-pack.md: usage, troubleshooting, and security considerations.

Testing & CI: Unit tests for the heuristics rules and scrubber added; an e2e workflow (.github/workflows/evidence-pack.yml) runs a smoke assembly against fixture data and validates size/encryption headers. I intentionally included simulated noisy alerts to assert the assembler prunes correctly. CI passed after a single rerun where a flaky external test (trace fixture) was adjusted.

Rollout plan: staged by team flag. Phase 1: enable for SRE and a small set of platform teams (2 weeks) to capture feedback and tweak heuristics. Phase 2: broader opt-in and metrics collection (evidence pack attach rate, median size, time-to-first-artifact). Fallback: if assembler fails or triggers size/security validation, incident-bot attaches a short diagnostic summary and logs the error for postmortem.

Checklist: 
- [x] Core assembler implemented + unit tests
- [x] Heuristics rule set + tests
- [x] Redaction and size enforcement integrated
- [x] Docs + ops config added
- [x] CI e2e workflow added
- [x] Feature-flag + staged rollout plan

Risks and mitigation: The main risk is overfitting heuristics to a narrow set of incidents and leaking sensitive fields. Mitigations: conservative default rules, rigorous redaction by registry, short retention, and a configurable opt-out.

Notes for reviewers: Focus on the rule definitions in heuristics.go (they are intentionally simple) and the scrubber integration in assembler.go. I included a couple of example evidence packs under ci/test/fixture/evidence.json for reference.

If merged, follow-up work tracked under ENG-4613 (adaptive heuristics tuning) and SRE-90 (evidence retention policy extension).

source.title: Proactive mitigations simulation harness and playbook safety gates
source.author: Ava Chen
source.release_notes: Add simulation harness for proactive mitigations and enforce safety gates on automated playbook runners. Includes tests and rollout guide.
source.body:
Motivation: On several recent incidents automated mitigations triggered by the incident-bot caused partial service disruption because there was no pre-run simulation of stateful actions and limited safety gating. This PR introduces a lightweight simulation harness that executes mitigation steps against a sandboxed state model and a set of declarative safety gates that must pass before the bot can apply real-world actions.

What this PR does (single-string description): Adds a mitigation simulation harness, a safety-gate DSL for playbooks, a test suite with representative incident scenarios, and small plumbing to record simulation traces in S3 for post-run analysis. Includes documentation, a canary rollout path, CLI helpers (incubate mode) and a feature flag to opt-in per-team.

Checklist: 
- [x] Simulation harness core runtime (deterministic, time-boxed) 
- [x] Safety-gate DSL and evaluator (thresholds, whitelists, dry-run-only exceptions) 
- [x] Unit and integration tests (coverage for 7 common mitigations) 
- [x] CI job adding simulation acceptance tests 
- [x] Documentation and README for playbook authors 
- [x] Opt-in feature flag and incremental rollout plan

Design notes: The harness runs a simulated environment that models key downstream state transitions: service replica counts, leader elections, routing-table updates, and external API rate limits. Mitigation steps implement a small SimulationAdapter interface (simulate(), apply()). Step authors implement simulate() to return a discrete list of state deltas; the harness composes deltas and checks safety gates before returning PASS/FAIL. Safety gates are declarative JSON with rules like max_service_down_time_seconds, max_restarts, and allowed_targets; the evaluator produces a human-readable report.

Backward compatibility: All existing playbooks will continue to work. By default the simulation runs in dry-run and does not block automated mitigations until the feature flag is enabled for a team. Safety gates default to permissive on opt-in, but the admin console will suggest recommended gate values based on historical incident telemetry.

Testing and verification: Added unit tests for gate evaluator and simulation engine. Added integration acceptance tests that run three synthetic incidents: noisy-restart-loop, partial-network-partition, and degraded-cache-eviction. CI runs the acceptance suite under the new workflow. Local repro: ./scripts/run-mitigation-sim.sh --scenario noisy-restart-loop --write-trace ./tmp/trace.json

Commits summary: See commits list below. High level: implement harness, implement gates DSL, wire feature flag, tests, docs, CI.

Changed files (high level): sources: incident-bot/sim/, incident-bot/playbooks/gates/, incident-bot/cli/, docs/playbooks/simulation.md, ci/workflows/sim-acceptance.yml, tests/fixtures/scenarios/*.json.

Rollout plan: Enable feature flag in staging for 2 weeks with trace collection. Run simulated mitigations on nightly canary incidents and review traces. After signoff enable opt-in in production for teams, then progressively require safety gates for fully automated mitigations.

Merge: squash-and-merge after 2 approvals and CI green.

Why this is different: Instead of only gating on simple dry-run checks, the harness executes a compact deterministic simulation of state transitions which catches class of races and mis-ordered steps that previously caused regressions. The design intentionally keeps the simulator conservative (no heavy modeling) to avoid flakiness and keep runs fast (~200–500ms per step in tests).
commits: c3b4f71  implement simulation harness core and adapter interface
a1d9b22  add safety-gate DSL and evaluator with JSON schemas
e5f8c56  implement CLI helpers and feature-flag wiring
b27d9a0  add acceptance tests and CI workflow for simulation
f90aa12  docs: playbooks simulation guide and rollout notes
d44b2be  fix: edge-case in gate evaluator for concurrent restarts
review_thread: Ravi Patel: Strong design. Please add one more unit test covering leader election churn. (requested changes)
Ava Chen: Added leader-election test and updated schema validators. Reply: PTAL
Sofia Morales: Can you add an example of a safety gate recommended value from historical data? (comment)
Ava Chen: Added guidance and script to compute suggested gates from telemetry. Reply: added in docs/playbooks/simulation.md
Jordan Kim: Verified CI acceptance run locally, approving. (approved)
ci_checks: sim-acceptance: pass (rerun 1x due to transient infra error) (610s total)
coverage: pass (new tests +3.1% on simulation modules)
notes: Follow-up work: wire simulated-trace ingestion into the runbook-recommender service (ENG-1499)
Ops: add CloudWatch alarm for unexpected simulation failures in production after rollout
Privacy: simulated traces exclude PII and are scrubbed before S3 writes
